expectations.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: TDD-referral-code-api.md exists in outputs/ directory

expectations.text: Document is at least 1500 words
expectations.evidence: Word count: 5051 words

expectations.text: Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Problem & context' covers consumer app, MAU, marketing deadline

expectations.text: Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
expectations.evidence: Section '2. Scope' has explicit 'In scope' and 'Out of scope' subsections

expectations.text: Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
expectations.evidence: Section '3. High-level approach' covers code lifecycle, redemption attribution, credit issuance with ASCII diagram

expectations.text: Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
expectations.evidence: Section '4. Detailed design' has 10 subsections (4a-4j) covering API surface, data access, latency, fraud, etc.

expectations.text: Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
expectations.evidence: Section '5. Alternatives considered' lists 6 alternatives each with rejection rationale (own microservice, Stripe Coupons, deterministic hashids, server-side attribution, synchronous credit, vendor)

expectations.text: Contains an NFRs / Non-functional requirements section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
expectations.evidence: Section '6. Non-functional requirements' includes 99.9% monthly availability, p95 120ms read/200ms write, 500 RPS burst, 4M MAU at 12 months

expectations.text: Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
expectations.evidence: Section '7. Risks & failure modes' enumerates 9 risks with likelihood, blast radius, mitigation, recovery

expectations.text: Contains a 'Rollout' / 'Migration' section
expectations.evidence: Section '8. Rollout & migration' has Phase 0-3 timeline, backwards compat, rollback story

expectations.text: Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
expectations.evidence: Section '9. Observability & operations' enumerates pages/tickets/daily, plus Observability NFR subsection with metrics, logs, traces, alerts table

expectations.text: Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
expectations.evidence: Section '10. Open questions' has 10 entries, 3 marked [BLOCKER]

expectations.text: Contains no fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems (only placeholders or PRD-mentioned references are acceptable)
expectations.evidence: Only placeholder '<link to referrals runbook — to be added>' and a path reference to the PRD file; no invented company URLs

expectations.text: TDD is structured as an API serving design (mentions endpoints/routes/methods, response shapes, AuthN/Z model)
expectations.evidence: Section 4a has full endpoint table with Method/Path/Auth/Purpose; section 4b explicitly covers AuthN (JWT) and AuthZ (row-level)

expectations.text: Defines a data model / schema for referral codes and redemption events with primary keys and important indexes
expectations.evidence: Section 4c includes SQL DDL for referrals.codes, referrals.redemptions, referrals.credit_receipts, referrals.stats with PRIMARY KEY and explicit indexes (redemptions_referrer_idx, redemptions_status_idx)

expectations.text: Explicitly addresses idempotency of the redemption call (idempotency key, dedup table, conditional write, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Redemption idempotent on PK (referee_user_id), INSERT ... ON CONFLICT pattern; credit_receipts table with idempotency_key column 'referral:activate:{redemption_id}:referrer'

expectations.text: Addresses fraud / self-referral / multi-account abuse prevention (referenced as design concern, not punted)
expectations.evidence: Section 4h 'Fraud guardrails' lists 4 explicit rules: self-network (device/IP/payment match), velocity per referrer, velocity per IP/device, trial-abuse signal

expectations.text: Defines or constrains what 'activation' means (e.g., first paid subscription start) and the credit-issuance trigger flow
expectations.evidence: Section 4g 'Credit issuance' details consumer for subscription.activated event with 5-step flow: lookup, fraud check, transactional update, ledger call, ack

expectations.text: Addresses code-collision / generation strategy (random + uniqueness check, deterministic from user ID, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Section 4c: 'secrets.token_bytes mapped to the alphabet and rely on the UNIQUE constraint to detect collisions; on conflict we retry up to 5 times. After 5 retries we widen to 7 chars'

expectations.text: Has a concrete latency budget for the API (numbers, not 'fast')
expectations.evidence: Section 4d: 'p95 ≤ 120 ms end-to-end for GET /referrals/me, p95 ≤ 200 ms for POST /v1/referrals/redemptions' with per-stage table (TLS 10ms, JWT 3ms, Redis 2ms, Postgres 20ms)

expectations.text: Discusses integration with Stripe and the internal credit ledger, including what happens if the credit write fails
expectations.evidence: Section 4g step 4-5 and 4i: 'If step 4 fails after step 3 committed: the receipts table records intent; on next event-redelivery (or manual retry via runbook) we re-issue using the same idempotency key'; ledger row in dependency table
